colors.saturation_level: highly saturated accents on dark background

animation_style.movement_quality: smooth, digital, snappy — spring-based with controlled overshoot
animation_style.easing_type: spring for entrances (snappy pops or smooth reveals), ease-out for exits
animation_style.complexity: simple but dynamic — every element has purposeful motion

entrance_types: spring scale up (snappy overshoot then settle)
staggered children (elements appear sequentially with slight delay)
draw-in for line/wave elements (line traces itself on screen)
neon flicker-on (rapid flashes then steady glow)
transitions: toggle switch slide for state changes
emphasis_effects: spring scale pulse (brief size bump then settle)
neon glow expansion (glow radius grows outward)
color flash green to white then back on key moments
loops: radiating signal arcs (opacity pulse outward with stagger)
subtle neon glow pulse (glow intensity oscillates)

entrance_methods: typewriter reveal for binary/code strings (character-by-character with blinking cursor)

scene_timing.sync_rule: All visual entrances are locked to narration. Text and elements appear on the exact syllable they represent.
scene_timing.entrance_duration: 300ms — from trigger to fully settled position, including any overshoot or spring settle
scene_timing.stagger_delay: 100ms between sequential elements in the same group
scene_timing.overshoot_constraint: Spring overshoot is a visual effect only. The element must reach its final resting state within the entrance_duration. No element should still be settling when the next narration beat fires.
scene_timing.effect: Creates a punchy, rhythmic tech-demo feel where visuals lock to the voiceover pace.

unique_visual_characteristics: neon green on dark mode aesthetic
notable_visual_patterns: green highlight color for active elements

colors.saturation_level: highly saturated accents on dark background

shapes.dimensionality: 2D flat with layered depth (background, mid-ground, foreground)

animation_style.movement_quality: smooth, digital, snappy — spring-based with controlled overshoot
animation_style.easing_type: spring for entrances (snappy pops or smooth reveals), ease-out for exits
animation_style.complexity: simple but dynamic — every element has purposeful motion

types_used: radial glow gradients behind neon elements
linear gradient on background for subtle depth
placement: behind key icons and text as neon glow halos
gradients.intensity: medium — visible but not overpowering, reinforces neon aesthetic
examples: radial glow halo fading from neon green to transparent
vertical gradient from dark to slightly lighter dark

text_effects.pop_in: elastic scale up from 0% to 110% then settle at 100%
text_effects.counter_scroll: digital odometer rolling effect for numbers increasing
text_effects.glitch_jitter: subtle horizontal displacement on frequency change

icon_behavior.bounce_on_entry: icons squash and stretch slightly upon appearance
icon_behavior.hover_state: slow, subtle vertical float for static icons
icon_behavior.pulse: rhythmic scaling of signal rings (opacity 100% to 0%)

character_micro_animations.tower_sway: imperceptible easing sway on antenna tips
character_micro_animations.blink_cursor: solid block cursor flashing at end of binary strings
character_micro_animations.sine_wave_modulation: frequency compression (accordion squeeze) when switching 4G to 5G

glow_and_light: neon green glow halos behind active elements
flicker on connect (rapid flashes then steady)
light leak overlay on scene transitions (green-tinted)

shadows.purpose: reinforce neon aesthetic, create depth on dark background

particles: small floating dots with slow drift for ambient depth
scatter particles on impact moments (burst outward from origin)
blur_types: subtle blur on background layers for depth separation
motion blur effect on fast-moving elements

background.type: solid dark with subtle radial gradient
background.complexity: minimal with ambient depth — visible grid lines or circuit traces
background.depth_layers: 2-3 layers (deep background, mid-ground glow halos, foreground elements)
background.animation: slow ambient drift on background particles, subtle gradient shift

scene_timing.sync_rule: All visual entrances are locked to narration. Text and elements appear on the exact syllable they represent.
scene_timing.entrance_duration: 300ms — from trigger to fully settled position, including any overshoot or spring settle
scene_timing.stagger_delay: 100ms between sequential elements in the same group
scene_timing.overshoot_constraint: Spring overshoot is a visual effect only. The element must reach its final resting state within the entrance_duration. No element should still be settling when the next narration beat fires.
scene_timing.effect: Creates a punchy, rhythmic tech-demo feel where visuals lock to the voiceover pace.

asset.instructions: Apply neon glow to all elements. Use palette colors for abstract elements. If an asset has an identity color (brand, recognizable object), preserve its identity color instead of palette. Use clean outlined icons with glow effects.

gradients.notes: Use radial gradients for glow halos behind elements. Use linear gradients for surface sheen on metallic or glass-like objects. Gradient stops should go from a bright palette color to transparent or to #0D0D0D.

stroke.color: palette bright color matching the fill
stroke.notes: Thin, clean outlines in a matching bright palette color. Strokes can have glow via feGaussianBlur filter.

shading.notes: Neon assets glow from within. Use radial gradients centered on the element: bright core fading to darker edges. This creates the signature neon-lit look.

complexity.notes: Clean shapes with glow effects. Not overly detailed — the glow is the visual richness. Internal structure should be visible but simplified.

filters.notes: Use feGaussianBlur for neon glow halos (blur 4-8px, placed behind the main shape). Use feDropShadow for colored glow beneath elements.
